2603.29559.title: When Can We Trust LLM Graders? Calibrating Confidence for Automated Assessment
2603.29559.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for automated grading, but their outputs can be unreliable. Rather than improving grading accuracy directly, we address a complementary problem: \textit{predicting when an LLM grader is likely to be correct}. This enables selective automation where high-confidence predictions are processed automatically while uncertain cases are flagged for human review. We compare three confidence estimation methods (self-reported confidence, self-consistency voting, and token probability) across seven LLMs of varying scale (4B to 120B parameters) on three educational datasets: RiceChem (long-answer chemistry), SciEntsBank, and Beetle (short-answer science). Our experiments reveal that self-reported confidence consistently achieves the best calibration across all conditions (avg ECE 0.166 vs 0.229 for self-consistency). Surprisingly, self-consistency remains 38\% worse despite requiring 5$\times$ the inference cost. Larger models exhibit substantially better calibration though gains vary by dataset and method (e.g., a 28\% ECE reduction for self-reported), with GPT-OSS-120B achieving the best calibration (avg ECE 0.100) and strong discrimination (avg AUC 0.668). We also observe that confidence is strongly top-skewed across methods, creating a ``confidence floor'' that practitioners must account for when setting thresholds. These findings suggest that simply asking LLMs to report their confidence provides a practical approach for identifying reliable grading predictions. Code is available \href{https://github.com/sonkar-lab/llm_grading_calibration}{here}.
2603.29559.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在自動評分方面顯示出潛力，但其輸出可能不可靠。與其直接改善評分準確性，我們解決了一個互補問題：\textit{預測何時LLM評分器可能是正確的}。這使得選擇性自動化成為可能，當高信心的預測可以自動處理，而不確定的案例則標記以供人類審查。我們比較了三種信心估計方法（自報信心、自一致性投票和標記概率），在七個不同規模的LLM（4B到120B參數）上，使用三個教育數據集：RiceChem（長答案化學）、SciEntsBank和Beetle（短答案科學）。我們的實驗顯示，自報信心在所有條件下始終實現最佳的校準（平均ECE 0.166對比自一致性的0.229）。令人驚訝的是，自一致性儘管需要5$\times$的推理成本，仍然差38\%。較大的模型顯示出顯著更好的校準，儘管增益因數據集和方法而異（例如，自報信心的ECE減少28\%），GPT-OSS-120B實現了最佳的校準（平均ECE 0.100）和強有力的區分能力（平均AUC 0.668）。我們還觀察到，信心在各種方法中強烈偏向高端，形成了實務工作者在設定閾值時必須考慮的“信心底線”。這些發現表明，僅僅要求LLMs報告其信心提供了一種實用的方法來識別可靠的評分預測。代碼可在\href{https://github.com/sonkar-lab/llm_grading_calibration}{這裡}獲得。
2603.29559.author: Robinson Ferrer et.al.
2603.29559.authors: Robinson Ferrer, Damla Turgut, Zhongzhou Chen, Shashank Sonkar
